methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ultima Genomics is a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Gilad Almogy, currently valued at $4.0B post-money on $600M total capital raised. The valuation analysis triangulates against a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public biotech comparables, all operating in the Biotech & Health sector at similar or adjacent funding stages.

Two quantitative methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x multiplier to the $4.0B last post-money valuation, implying $6.8B forward value based on typical Series C advancement dynamics. Peer-median anchoring examined 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies—including Insitro ($2.4B Series C), HistoSonics ($2.2B Series D), and Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B Series B)—yielding a median valuation of $1.7B, with observed range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (7.4x median across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) could not be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically withhold.

The triangulation of these two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $7.8B, with a median of $4.2B. The current $4.0B valuation sits near this median estimate but reflects substantial tension between peer-median comparables ($1.7B) and stage-progression forecasting ($6.8B). Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory (unknowable from public data), capital efficiency relative to disclosed peer benchmarks, and the target's relative positioning on clinical/product development timelines within its peer cohort.
